summary: Bravo Logistics Ltd. (CUST-002), an enterprise customer, reports via chat that webhook deliveries have been down for ~6 hours, blocking their entire logistics operation. Classified as a high-urgency bug report (confidence 0.95). This is service-impacting and appears related to an existing open ticket (TCK-0873) about webhook delivery delays. Full context (customer record, SLA policy, escalation policy, and bug-handling SOP) was retrieved successfully.
customer_context: CUST-002 is Bravo Logistics Ltd. (ops@bravologistics.example.com), an enterprise-tier customer with 41 months tenure. They have an active annual Enterprise plan order (ORD-7210, 250 seats, $24,000). Past tickets include an OPEN bug report TCK-0873 (webhook deliveries delayed during peak hours, opened 2026-06-28) and a resolved SSO/Okta feature request (TCK-0512). The current report is likely a continuation or escalation of the ongoing webhook issue in TCK-0873.

suggested_actions.action: Send a first response within 4 business hours as required for high-urgency enterprise customers per POL-SLA-001, acknowledging the webhook outage and requesting reproduction details, affected environment, and impact scope per SOP-BUG-001.
Before filing a new engineering ticket, check the known-issues list and link this report to the existing open ticket TCK-0873 (webhook delivery delays) per SOP-BUG-001.

Escalate this confirmed service-impacting webhook outage to engineering within 1 business day and route directly to the customer's dedicated enterprise account team, per POL-ESCALATION-001 and POL-SLA-001.
Ensure the customer receives a status update at least every 48 hours while this high-urgency ticket remains open, per POL-SLA-001.

classification.reason: The customer reports webhook deliveries have been down for hours, a service-impacting failure of documented functionality. It is blocking their operations, making it high urgency.
